Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx

Class Flixel

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.Flixel
public final class Flixel extends Object

The static singleton entry point and global manager for the FlixelGDX framework. This class exposes core services, settings, and utility methods needed to develop games and interactive applications using FlixelGDX. Nearly all main gameplay logic interacts with Flixel via this class, either to control the playback loop, switch states/scenes, access global systems (input, audio, asset management, logging, debugging), or modify global properties.

Core Responsibilities

  • State Management: Switches between FlixelState instances to manage major scenes in your game.
  • Input Handling: Provides access to the keyboard manager (keys) for polling key states and input events.
  • Sound System: Exposes a global sound manager for playing music and sound effects.
  • Asset Loading: Offers a unified assets interface for loading, caching, and retrieving textures, sounds, and data.
  • Logging and Debugging: Centralizes log output through log, and supplies tools for in-game watches and performance tracking.
  • Reflection Utility: Simplifies cross-platform field and method access with reflect.
  • Camera and Drawing Context: Handles the active camera selection and global antialiasing options.
  • Signals and Events: Emits signals for state switches, updates, and critical events.
  • Frame timers: FlixelTimer.getGlobalManager() is stepped from FlixelGame. Use FlixelTimer.wait(float, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer.FlixelTimerListener) or start(...) on the manager. getTimeScale() scales timer elapsed only (not the whole game loop).

Typical Usage


 // Switch states.
 Flixel.switchState(new MyGameState());

 // Play a sound.
 Flixel.sound.play("explosion.mp3");

 // Check if a key is pressed.
 if (Flixel.keys.justPressed(FlixelKeys.SPACE)) {
   // Jump!
 }

 // Check if a mouse button is pressed.
 if (Flixel.mouse.justPressed(FlixelMouseButton.LEFT)) {
   // Left mouse button was just pressed!
 }

 // Log diagnostic information.
 Flixel.info("Player has reached checkpoint.");
 Flixel.warn("Player is low on health.");
 Flixel.error("Game crashed!");

 // Load an asset.
 Flixel.assets.load("player.png");

 // Use the reflection utility.
 if (Flixel.reflect.hasField(player, "health")) {
   player.health = 100;
 }

 // Use the global signal system.
 Flixel.Signals.preStateSwitch.add(data -> {
   Flixel.info("Now switching to state: " + data.state().toString());
 });

Design Notes

  • The Flixel class is not meant to be instantiated. It should be interacted with strictly via its static fields and methods.
  • Custom configuration and subsystems can be plugged in by replacing or augmenting the static references, e.g., custom FlixelLogger or FlixelStackTraceProvider for advanced logging.
  • All engine systems are globally accessible through this class to simplify game logic implementation.

Threading

All Flixel APIs, unless otherwise noted, are intended to be called from the main libGDX rendering thread.

Lifecycle

The Flixel singleton is initialized by the internal game bootstrap sequence. Applications should not attempt to reinitialize or replace this class directly.

    Contains all the global events that get dispatched when something happens in the game.

  • Field Details

    • keys

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelKeyInputManager keys
      Keyboard input manager. Use Flixel.keys.pressed(key), Flixel.keys.justPressed(key), etc.

    • sound

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelAudioManager sound
      Central audio manager. Use Flixel.sound.play(), Flixel.sound.playMusic(), etc.

    • assets

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelAssetManager assets
      Central asset manager. Use this for loading, caching and managing assets.

    • watch

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelDebugWatchManager watch
      The debug watch manager. Access via Flixel.watch.add(...), Flixel.watch.remove(...), etc.

    • save

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelSave save
      Preferences-based save helper. Call FlixelSave.bind(String, String) before use.

    • mouse

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelMouseManager mouse
      Mouse/pointer input manager. Use after initialize(FlixelGame).

    • log

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelLogger log
      The default logger used by info(java.lang.Object), warn(java.lang.Object), and error(java.lang.String).

    • reflect

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelReflection reflect
      Global reflection service. Use Flixel.reflect.hasField(target, fieldName), etc.

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public static void initialize(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelGame gameInstance)
      Initializes the entire Flixel system.

      This gets called BEFORE FlixelGame.create() is executed. It sets up every core system that Flixel needs to work, such as FlixelAssetManager, audio system, key input manager, logger, backend systems for different platforms, and more.

      Normally called once at startup. After resetGame(), initialized is cleared and this may run again to rebuild global subsystems on the same FlixelGame instance.

      Parameters:
      gameInstance - The FlixelGame instance to use.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If Flixel has already been initialized.

    • setAlerter

      public static void setAlerter(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelAlerter alertSystem)
      Sets the system used for displaying alert notifications to the user.

      This must be set before initialize(FlixelGame). Calling it after initialization throws an exception.

    • setStackTraceProvider

      public static void setStackTraceProvider(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelStackTraceProvider provider)
      Sets the system used for providing stack traces on logs.

      This must be set before initialize(FlixelGame). Calling it after initialization throws an exception.

    • switchState

      public static void switchState(FlixelState newState)
      Sets the current state to the provided state, triggers garbage collection and clears all active tweens by default.
      Parameters:
      newState - The new FlixelState to set as the current state.

    • switchState

      public static void switchState(FlixelState newState, boolean clearTweens)
      Sets the current state to the provided state and triggers Java's garbage collector for memory cleanup.
      Parameters:
      newState - The new FlixelState to set as the current state.
      clearTweens - Should all active tweens be cancelled and their pools be cleared?

    • switchState

      public static void switchState(FlixelState newState, boolean clearTweens, boolean triggerGC)
      Sets the current state to the provided state.
      Parameters:
      newState - The new FlixelState to set as the current state.
      clearTweens - Should all active tweens be cancelled and their pools be cleared?
      triggerGC - Should Java's garbage collector be triggered for memory cleanup?

    • switchState

      public static void switchState(FlixelState newState, boolean clearTweens, boolean triggerGC, Supplier<FlixelState> stateFactory)
      Sets the current state to the provided state.
      Parameters:
      newState - The new FlixelState to set as the current state.
      clearTweens - Should all active tweens be cancelled and their pools be cleared?
      triggerGC - Should Java's garbage collector be triggered for memory cleanup?
      stateFactory - The factory to use to create a new state instance when resetState() is called.

    • showInfoAlert

      public static void showInfoAlert(String title, String message)
      Shows an info alert notification to the user.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the alert.
      message - The message of the alert.

    • showWarningAlert

      public static void showWarningAlert(String title, String message)
      Shows a warning alert notification to the user.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the alert.
      message - The message of the alert.

    • showErrorAlert

      public static void showErrorAlert(String title, String message)
      Shows an error alert notification to the user.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the alert.
      message - The message of the alert.

    • info

      public static void info(Object message)
      Logs a generic informational message. This is likely the method you'll use the most, as it's for general messages that don't fit into the other log methods.
      Parameters:
      message - The message to log.

    • info

      public static void info(String tag, Object message)
      Logs a generic informational message with a custom tag. This is likely the method you'll use the most, as it's for general messages that don't fit into the other log methods.
      Parameters:
      tag - The tag to log the message under.
      message - The message to log.

    • warn

      public static void warn(Object message)
      Logs a generic warning message. This is for messages that are not errors, but are still important to note.
      Parameters:
      message - The message to log.

    • warn

      public static void warn(String tag, Object message)
      Logs a warning message, with yellow highlighting, with a custom tag. This is for messages that are not errors, but are still important to note.
      Parameters:
      tag - The tag to log the message under.
      message - The message to log.

    • error

      public static void error(String message)
      Logs a error message, with red highlighting (and the file location underlined), with a custom tag. This is for events that are typically not recoverable.
      Parameters:
      message - The message to log.

    • error

      public static void error(String tag, Object message)
      Logs a error message, with red highlighting (and the file location underlined), with a custom tag. This is for events that are typically not recoverable.
      Parameters:
      tag - The tag to log the message under.
      message - The message to log.

    • error

      public static void error(String tag, Object message, Throwable throwable)
      Logs a error message, with red highlighting (and the file location underlined), with a custom tag. This is for events that are typically not recoverable.
      Parameters:
      tag - The tag to log the message under.
      message - The message to log.
      throwable - The throwable to log.

    • setLogger

      public static void setLogger(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelLogger logger)

    • setDefaultLogTag

      public static void setDefaultLogTag(@NotNull @NotNull String tag)

    • setLogsFolder

      public static void setLogsFolder(@Nullable @Nullable String absolutePathToLogsFolder)
      Sets the folder where log files are stored, or null for default (project root in IDE, next to JAR when run from JAR).
      Parameters:
      absolutePathToLogsFolder - The absolute path to the logs folder, or null for default.

    • getLogsFolder

      public static String getLogsFolder()
      Returns the custom logs folder path, or null if using the default.

    • setCanStoreLogs

      public static void setCanStoreLogs(boolean canStoreLogs)
      Enables or disables writing logs to a file when startFileLogging() is called.
      Parameters:
      canStoreLogs - true to enable file logging, false to disable.

    • canStoreLogs

      public static boolean canStoreLogs()
      Returns whether file logging is enabled for the default logger.
      Returns:
      true if file logging is enabled, false otherwise.

    • setMaxLogFiles

      public static void setMaxLogFiles(int maxLogFiles)
      Sets the maximum number of log files to keep.
      Parameters:
      maxLogFiles - The maximum number of log files to keep.

    • getMaxLogFiles

      public static int getMaxLogFiles()
      Returns the maximum number of log files to keep. If no logger is set, returns 0.
      Returns:
      The maximum number of log files to keep.

    • startFileLogging

      public static void startFileLogging()
      Starts file logging for the default logger (uses its current `canStoreLogs` and `maxLogFiles`).

    • stopFileLogging

      public static void stopFileLogging()
      Stops the default logger's file writer thread; call during game shutdown.

    • getAlerter

      public static FlixelAlerter getAlerter()

    • getLogger

      public static FlixelLogger getLogger()

    • getLogMode

      public static FlixelLogMode getLogMode()

    • ensureAssets

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelAssetManager ensureAssets()
      Ensures assets is available for embedded libGDX usage.

      If Flixel has not been initialized yet, this creates a default asset manager on first use. Note that audio loaders are only registered once the global audio system is initialized.

    • getGame

      public static FlixelGame getGame()

    • getStage

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.scenes.scene2d.Stage getStage()

    • getState

      public static FlixelState getState()

    • getMusic

      public static FlixelSound getMusic()

    • getWindowSize

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 getWindowSize()

    • getWindowWidth

      public static int getWindowWidth()

    • getWindowHeight

      public static int getWindowHeight()

    • getViewSize

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 getViewSize()

    • getViewWidth

      public static int getViewWidth()

    • getViewHeight

      public static int getViewHeight()

    • getAudioEngine

      public static games.rednblack.miniaudio.MiniAudio getAudioEngine()

    • getMasterVolume

      public static float getMasterVolume()

    • getElapsed

      public static float getElapsed()
      Returns the capped elapsed time (in seconds) for the current frame. This value is clamped between FlixelConstants.Graphics.MIN_ELAPSED and FlixelConstants.Graphics.MAX_ELAPSED by FlixelGame each frame.

    • getTimeScale

      public static float getTimeScale()
      Global timer scale. Multiplied into FlixelTimer updates each frame.

    • setTimeScale

      public static void setTimeScale(float scale)
      Sets the global timer scale. Non-finite or negative values are clamped to 0f.
      Parameters:
      scale - The scale to set.

    • setDebugMode

      public static void setDebugMode(boolean enabled)
      Sets whether the game is running in debug mode. This may only be called once, typically from the platform launcher before the game starts. A second call throws an IllegalStateException.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to enable debug mode.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If called more than once.

    • isDebugMode

      public static boolean isDebugMode()

    • setRuntimeMode

      public static void setRuntimeMode(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelRuntimeMode mode)
      Sets the runtime mode for the game. This may only be called once, typically from the platform launcher before the game starts. A second call throws an IllegalStateException.
      Parameters:
      mode - The FlixelRuntimeMode to set.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If called more than once.

    • getRuntimeMode

      public static FlixelRuntimeMode getRuntimeMode()
      Returns the current runtime mode. Defaults to FlixelRuntimeMode.RELEASE.

    • getDebugToggleKey

      public static int getDebugToggleKey()
      Returns the key used to toggle the debug overlay visibility.
      See Also:
      • FlixelKey

    • setDebugToggleKey

      public static void setDebugToggleKey(int key)
      Changes the key used to toggle the debug overlay visibility.
      Parameters:
      key - A key constant from FlixelKey.

    • getDebugDrawToggleKey

      public static int getDebugDrawToggleKey()
      Returns the key used to toggle visual debug (bounding box drawing) on/off.
      See Also:
      • FlixelKey

    • setDebugDrawToggleKey

      public static void setDebugDrawToggleKey(int key)
      Changes the key used to toggle visual debug (bounding box drawing) on/off.
      Parameters:
      key - A key constant from FlixelKey.

    • getDebugPauseKey

      public static int getDebugPauseKey()
      Key used to pause the game update loop (debug mode only).

    • setDebugPauseKey

      public static void setDebugPauseKey(int key)

    • getDebugCameraPanButton

      public static int getDebugCameraPanButton()
      Mouse button (e.g. Input.Buttons.RIGHT) for debug camera pan while paused.

    • setDebugCameraPanButton

      public static void setDebugCameraPanButton(int button)

    • getDebugCameraCycleLeftKey

      public static int getDebugCameraCycleLeftKey()

    • setDebugCameraCycleLeftKey

      public static void setDebugCameraCycleLeftKey(int key)

    • getDebugCameraCycleRightKey

      public static int getDebugCameraCycleRightKey()

    • setDebugCameraCycleRightKey

      public static void setDebugCameraCycleRightKey(int key)

    • isPaused

      public static boolean isPaused()
      Whether the game update loop is frozen (debug pause).
      See Also:
      • FlixelGame.setGamePaused(boolean)

    • setPaused

      public static void setPaused(boolean paused)

    • resetState

      @Deprecated public static void resetState(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelState newRoot)
      Deprecated.
      Use switchState(FlixelState).
      Same as switchState(FlixelState). Prefer switchState directly; this method exists for older call sites.

    • resetState

      public static void resetState()
      Refreshes the current state by creating a new instance from the factory last set by switchState(FlixelState, boolean, boolean, Supplier). Does nothing if the factory is null.

      This is the equivalent of calling Flixel.switchState(new CurrentState()).

    • resetGame

      public static void resetGame()
      Full session teardown. Sets initialized to false, destroys audio and tears down the current FlixelGame via FlixelGame.reset() (stage/batch/state/world only, not a full FlixelGame.destroy() application shutdown) and clears cameras/state/debug references.

      Re-initializes the same FlixelGame instance passed to the first initialize(FlixelGame). libGDX keeps the original ApplicationListener reference (e.g. Lwjgl3Application(game, ...)); allocating a new FlixelGame would leave rendering on a dead instance and break statics (null batch, etc.).

      Unlike resetState(), this is intended for a cold restart from code. Call on the main libGDX thread. The window keeps running. This method does not call Gdx.app.exit().

    • setDebugOverlay

      public static void setDebugOverlay(@NotNull @NotNull Supplier<FlixelDebugOverlay> factory)
      Sets a factory that produces the FlixelDebugOverlay used when debug mode is enabled. This can be called either in the launcher (before the game starts) or in the FlixelGame.create() method itself.

      A factory is used instead of a new instance directly for timing, so that way the debug overlay can be set even before GL context is created.

      Example: 

      
 Flixel.setDebugOverlay(MyCustomOverlay::new);
      Parameters:
      factory - A supplier that creates a new FlixelDebugOverlay (or subclass).

    • getDebugOverlay

      public static FlixelDebugOverlay getDebugOverlay()
      Returns the active debug overlay instance, or null when debug mode is off or the overlay has not been created yet.

    • getJavaHeapUsedBytes

      public static long getJavaHeapUsedBytes()
      Returns the Java heap memory currently in use, in bytes.
      Returns:
      The Java heap memory currently in use, in bytes.

    • getJavaHeapUsedMegabytes

      public static float getJavaHeapUsedMegabytes()
      Returns the Java heap memory currently in use, in megabytes.
      Returns:
      The Java heap memory currently in use, in megabytes.

    • getJavaHeapUsedGigabytes

      public static float getJavaHeapUsedGigabytes()
      Returns the Java heap memory currently in use, in gigabytes.
      Returns:
      The Java heap memory currently in use, in gigabytes.

    • getJavaHeapTotalBytes

      public static long getJavaHeapTotalBytes()
      Returns the total Java heap memory allocated by the JVM, in bytes.
      Returns:
      The total Java heap memory allocated by the JVM, in bytes.

    • getJavaHeapTotalMegabytes

      public static float getJavaHeapTotalMegabytes()
      Returns the total Java heap memory allocated by the JVM, in megabytes.
      Returns:
      The total Java heap memory allocated by the JVM, in megabytes.

    • getJavaHeapTotalGigabytes

      public static float getJavaHeapTotalGigabytes()
      Returns the total Java heap memory allocated by the JVM, in gigabytes.
      Returns:
      The total Java heap memory allocated by the JVM, in gigabytes.

    • getJavaHeapMaxBytes

      public static long getJavaHeapMaxBytes()
      Returns the maximum Java heap memory available to the JVM, in bytes.
      Returns:
      The maximum Java heap memory available to the JVM, in bytes.

    • getJavaHeapMaxMegabytes

      public static float getJavaHeapMaxMegabytes()
      Returns the maximum Java heap memory available to the JVM, in megabytes.
      Returns:
      The maximum Java heap memory available to the JVM, in megabytes.

    • getJavaHeapMaxGigabytes

      public static float getJavaHeapMaxGigabytes()
      Returns the maximum Java heap memory available to the JVM, in gigabytes.
      Returns:
      The maximum Java heap memory available to the JVM, in gigabytes.

    • getNativeHeapUsedBytes

      public static long getNativeHeapUsedBytes()
      Returns an estimate of the native heap usage in bytes as reported by libGDX. This is not available on all platforms and may return 0 when unsupported.

    • getNativeHeapUsedMegabytes

      public static float getNativeHeapUsedMegabytes()
      Returns the native heap usage in megabytes.
      Returns:
      The native heap usage in megabytes.

    • getNativeHeapUsedGigabytes

      public static float getNativeHeapUsedGigabytes()
      Returns the native heap usage in gigabytes.
      Returns:
      The native heap usage in gigabytes.

    • getApproximateLoadedTextureBytes

      public static long getApproximateLoadedTextureBytes()
      Approximate GPU-style memory for loaded textures reachable from the global assets AssetManager: each Texture is counted at most once (deduplicated), including page textures referenced by managed TextureAtlas instances.

      Estimate is width x height x bytesPerPixel per distinct Texture (base level, uncompressed). Excludes framebuffers, mip level overhead, compressed GPU formats (heuristic bpp), and any texture not registered with the asset manager. Use for debug/trending only.

      Uses internal synchronized scratch buffers; does not allocate per call.

      Returns:
      Sum in bytes, or 0 when assets is not initialized.

    • getFPS

      public static int getFPS()
      Returns the current frames-per-second as reported by the graphics backend.
      Returns:
      The current frames-per-second as reported by the graphics backend.

    • getCamera

      public static FlixelCamera getCamera()

    • getCameras

      public static FlixelCamera[] getCameras()

    • getCamerasArray

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<FlixelCamera> getCamerasArray()

    • addCamera

      public static void addCamera(FlixelCamera camera)

    • getDrawCamera

      @Nullable public static @Nullable FlixelCamera getDrawCamera()
      The camera currently being drawn in FlixelGame.draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch), or null if not in a camera pass.

    • isOnDrawCamera

      public static boolean isOnDrawCamera(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelCamera[] cameras)
      Whether something with the given cameras list should render during the current draw pass. null or an empty array means all cameras; otherwise, the object is drawn only if getDrawCamera() is reference-equal to an entry.

    • isFullscreen

      public static boolean isFullscreen()

    • getPlatform

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.Application.ApplicationType getPlatform()

    • getStackTraceProvider

      public static FlixelStackTraceProvider getStackTraceProvider()

    • setReflection

      public static void setReflection(FlixelReflection reflection)
      Sets the runtime reflection service implementation.
      Parameters:
      reflection - Reflection service to expose as reflect.

    • getVersion

      public static String getVersion()
      Returns the version of the FlixelGDX library.

      The version is read from a version.properties file in the module `.jar` file, where it is defined as version=<version>. If the file is not found, or the version is not defined, the method returns "Unknown", although this should never happen in theory.

      Returns:
      The version of the FlixelGDX library.

    • setLogMode

      public static void setLogMode(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelLogMode mode)

    • globalAntialiasing

      public static boolean globalAntialiasing()

    • setAntialiasing

      public static void setAntialiasing(boolean enabled)

    • getWorld

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.physics.box2d.World getWorld()

    • setGravity

      public static void setGravity(float gravity)
      Sets the gravity of the global Box2D world. Convenience overload that sets horizontal gravity to 0.
      Parameters:
      gravity - Vertical gravity in m/s² (negative = down in most setups).

    • setGravity

      public static void setGravity(float gravityX, float gravityY)
      Sets the gravity of the global Box2D world.
      Parameters:
      gravityX - Horizontal gravity in m/s².
      gravityY - Vertical gravity in m/s².

    • getGravity

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 getGravity()
      Returns the current gravity of the Box2D world, or (0,0) if no world exists.

    • getWorldBounds

      public static float[] getWorldBounds()
      Returns the world bounds used for collision broad-phase culling. The returned array is [x, y, width, height].

    • setWorldBounds

      public static void setWorldBounds(float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Sets the world bounds used for collision culling.
      Parameters:
      x - Left edge of the world.
      y - Top edge of the world.
      width - Width of the world in pixels.
      height - Height of the world in pixels.

    • overlap

      public static boolean overlap(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup1, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup2, @Nullable @Nullable BiConsumer<FlixelObject,FlixelObject> notifyCallback, @Nullable @Nullable BiFunction<FlixelObject,FlixelObject,Boolean> processCallback)
      Checks for overlaps between two objects or groups. Can be called with any combination of single FlixelObjects and FlixelGroupables.
      Parameters:
      objectOrGroup1 - First object or group (may be null to use the current state).
      objectOrGroup2 - Second object or group (may be null to use the current state).
      notifyCallback - Called for each overlapping pair. May be null.
      processCallback - If provided, must return true for the pair to count as overlapping. Pass null for simple AABB overlap.
      Returns:
      true if any overlaps were detected.

    • overlap

      public static boolean overlap(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup1, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup2)
      Shorthand for overlap(FlixelBasic, FlixelBasic, BiConsumer, BiFunction) with no callbacks.

    • collide

      public static boolean collide(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup1, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup2, @Nullable @Nullable BiConsumer<FlixelObject,FlixelObject> notifyCallback)
      Checks for overlaps and separates colliding objects. Equivalent to calling overlap(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, java.util.function.BiConsumer<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject>, java.util.function.BiFunction<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, java.lang.Boolean>) with FlixelObject.separate(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject) as the process callback.
      Parameters:
      objectOrGroup1 - First object or group.
      objectOrGroup2 - Second object or group.
      notifyCallback - Called for each pair that was separated. May be null.
      Returns:
      true if any objects were separated.

    • collide

      public static boolean collide(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup1, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelBasic objectOrGroup2)
      Shorthand for collide(FlixelBasic, FlixelBasic, BiConsumer) with no notifyCallback.
